title: Smoky Crunchy Mexican Chicken Bowl
description: A diabetic-friendly Mexican bowl bursting with smoky flavors, crunchy tortilla chips, and fresh coriander, all pressure-cooked in under 30 minutes.
instructions: Heat the pressure cooker over medium heat and add olive oil.
Add the diced chicken and sear until lightly browned, about 3 minutes.
Stir in chipotle powder, cumin, paprika, and minced garlic; cook until fragrant, 30 seconds.
Pour in the low‑sodium chicken broth, season with salt and pepper, seal the pressure cooker, and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
Quick‑release the pressure, then stir in the chopped coriander leaves.
In a small skillet, lightly toast the crushed tortilla chips until golden and crisp, or use pre‑toasted chips.
Divide the chicken mixture into four bowls, top each with crunchy chips, and squeeze fresh lime juice over.
Garnish with extra coriander leaves if desired and serve immediately.
